title: add "chmod -R a+rx" to podman-pull code when using additionalImageStores and the "--root=" option
body:
### Feature request description

Every time a user pulls an image to an additionalImageStore location using "podman --root=<pathToAddlImageStore> pull ...", in order for any other user on the system to access the image they need to chmod -R a+rx <pathToAddlImageStore> afterwards. If this step is missed, then every other non-root podman user on the system is unable to run any podman command unless they modify their personal storage.conf to remove the additionalimagestores line. Instead, they get an error "...open /overlay-images/images.json: permission denied". For a large multi-user Linux server, this is a big problem.

I respectfully request that the chmod be integrated into "podman --root= pull..." so it cannot be missed.

### Suggest potential solution

The default for a rootless "podman --root=<path> pull <image>" should be an automatic "chmod -R a+rx <path>" to ensure other rootless users have access to the shared image store.  For those times where the rootless users do not want to share their additional ImageStore, an additional option or switch could be implemented to avoid the chmod.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

The current manual chmod -R a+rx workflow is errorprone.  We can use a script to automate it, but this seems like a kludge.  Alternatively, the podman code could set an appropriate umask when pulling to an additionalImageStore, perhaps based on storage.conf setting "force_mask=shared"?  

### Additional context

I am using RHEL 8.8 with the built-in Podman 4.4.x

comments.body: Your best chance for feature consideration on RHEL is to file the RFE through official RHEL channels, like bugzilla, etc.  Is that something you are willing to do?
This feature is needed also to `podman build --pull <PATH>`.
